annotations.label: Abelian category (informal)

anchor.snippet: an abelian category is a category in which morphisms and objects can be added

annotations.note: `CategoryTheory.Abelian` is the standard Mathlib formalization of abelian categories.

annotations.label: Category of abelian groups Ab

anchor.snippet: The motivating prototypical example of an abelian category is the category of abelian groups

annotations.note: An `Abelian` instance is registered on `AddCommGrpCat`.

annotations.label: Abelian category (axiomatic)

anchor.snippet: A category is abelian if it is preadditive and

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Abelian` class extends `Preadditive`, `IsNormalMonoCategory`, `IsNormalEpiCategory` and assumes (co)kernels and a zero object.

annotations.label: Preadditive category

anchor.snippet: A category is preadditive if it is enriched over the monoidal category

annotations.note: `CategoryTheory.Preadditive` axiomatizes the Ab-enrichment via add/neg structure on each Hom.

annotations.label: Additive category

anchor.snippet: A preadditive category is additive if every finite set of objects has a biproduct

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated `Additive` class; instead it uses `Preadditive C` + `HasFiniteBiproducts C` jointly.

annotations.label: Preabelian category

anchor.snippet: An additive category is preabelian if every morphism has both a kernel and a cokernel

annotations.note: No `Preabelian` class exists; the conditions appear as `Preadditive` + `HasKernels` + `HasCokernels` (+ biproducts) ingredients of `Abelian`.

annotations.label: Abelian category (piecemeal)

anchor.snippet: a preabelian category is abelian if every monomorphism and every epimorphism is normal

annotations.note: Mathlib's definition exactly extends `IsNormalMonoCategory` and `IsNormalEpiCategory` over a preadditive category.

annotations.label: Finitely generated and finite abelian groups

anchor.snippet: The category of all finitely generated abelian groups is also an abelian category

annotations.note: No `Abelian` instance on a category of finitely generated abelian groups was found.

annotations.label: Modules over a ring

anchor.snippet: then the category of all left (or right) modules over R is an abelian category

annotations.note: `ModuleCat.abelian` provides the `Abelian (ModuleCat R)` instance.

annotations.label: Mitchell's embedding theorem

anchor.snippet: any small abelian category is equivalent to a full subcategory of such a category of modules

annotations.note: The Freyd–Mitchell embedding theorem is proved as `freyd_mitchell` for any abelian category (via `AsSmall`).

annotations.label: Finitely generated modules over noetherian ring

anchor.snippet: then the category of finitely generated left modules over R is abelian

annotations.note: No `Abelian` instance for a category of finitely generated modules over a noetherian ring was located.

annotations.label: Vector spaces over a field

anchor.snippet: the category of vector spaces over a fixed field k is abelian

annotations.note: The `Abelian (ModuleCat R)` instance specializes to `R` a field, giving abelianness of vector spaces.

annotations.label: Vector bundles (non-example)

anchor.snippet: then the category of all (real or complex) vector bundles on X is not usually an abelian category

annotations.note: No formalization of vector bundles forming (or failing to form) an abelian category was found.

annotations.label: Sheaves of abelian groups

anchor.snippet: then the category of all sheaves of abelian groups on X is an abelian category

annotations.note: `sheafIsAbelian` provides `Abelian (Sheaf J D)` whenever `D` is abelian with appropriate (co)limits.

annotations.label: Functor category into an abelian category

anchor.snippet: then the category of all functors from C to A forms an abelian category

annotations.note: `functorCategoryAbelian` registers `Abelian (C ⥤ D)` when `D` is abelian.

annotations.label: Grothendieck axioms AB3, AB4, AB5

anchor.snippet: For every indexed family ( A i ) of objects of A , the coproduct

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes AB4 (exact coproducts) and AB5 (exact filtered colimits) via `HasExactColimitsOfShape`, with AB3 corresponding to existence of arbitrary colimits.

annotations.label: Dual axioms AB3*

anchor.snippet: For every indexed family ( A i ) of objects of A , the product

annotations.note: AB3* (existence of all products) is captured via the standard `HasProducts`/`HasLimits` machinery used throughout `GrothendieckAxioms`.

annotations.label: Axiom AB4*

anchor.snippet: the product of a family of epimorphisms is an epimorphism

annotations.note: AB4* is captured by `HasExactLimitsOfShape (Discrete α)`, the dual of AB4.

annotations.label: Axiom AB5*

anchor.snippet: filtered limits of exact sequences are exact

annotations.note: Cofiltered exactness AB5* is captured by `HasExactLimitsOfShape J` for `IsCofiltered J` (the `AB5Star`-style statement in the file).

annotations.label: Axioms AB1 and AB2

anchor.snippet: Axioms AB1) and AB2) were also given

annotations.note: AB1 (kernels/cokernels exist) and AB2 (coimage→image is iso) are subsumed by being abelian, which Mathlib's `Abelian` class provides.

annotations.label: Axiom AB6

anchor.snippet: given a family of filtered categories

annotations.note: No formalization of Grothendieck's AB6 axiom on commuting families of filtered colimits was found.

annotations.label: Axiom AB6*

anchor.snippet: given a family of cofiltered categories

annotations.note: No formalization of AB6* was found.

annotations.label: Zero morphism

anchor.snippet: there is a special zero morphism from A to B

annotations.note: `HasZeroMorphisms` axiomatizes the canonical zero morphism between any two objects.

annotations.label: Epi-mono factorization

anchor.snippet: every morphism f can be written as the composition of an epimorphism followed by a monomorphism

annotations.note: `Abelian.imageFactorisation` gives the canonical epi–mono image factorisation in any abelian category.

annotations.label: Subobjects form a bounded lattice

anchor.snippet: the poset of subobjects of any given object A is a bounded lattice

annotations.note: `Subobject` carries `Lattice` and `BoundedOrder` instances (and even `CompleteLattice`).

annotations.label: Abelian category as module over f.g. abelian groups

anchor.snippet: Every abelian category A is a module over the monoidal category of finitely generated abelian groups

annotations.note: No formalization of the monoidal-module structure of an abelian category over finitely generated abelian groups was found.

annotations.label: Flatness in an abelian category

anchor.snippet: flatness refers to the idea that

annotations.note: Flatness for objects of a general abelian category (via tensor with the f.g. abelian group action) is not formalized in `CategoryTheory.Abelian`.

annotations.label: Five lemma and snake lemma in abelian categories

anchor.snippet: Important theorems that apply in all abelian categories include the five lemma

annotations.note: The snake lemma is `SnakeInput.snake_lemma` and the five lemma is in `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.DiagramLemmas.Four`.

annotations.label: Semi-simple abelian category

anchor.snippet: is called semi-simple if there is a collection of objects

annotations.note: No general `IsSemisimpleCategory` class for abelian categories was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Module category semi-simple iff R semisimple

anchor.snippet: most module categories over a ring

annotations.note: While `IsSemisimpleRing`/`IsSemisimpleModule` are in Mathlib, no equivalence with semi-simplicity of `ModuleCat R` as an abelian category was found.

annotations.label: Vector spaces are semi-simple

anchor.snippet: The category of vector spaces

annotations.note: No abelian-category-level statement of semi-simplicity of `ModuleCat k` for a field `k` was located.

annotations.label: Maschke's theorem

anchor.snippet: By Maschke's theorem the category of representations

annotations.note: Maschke's theorem is proved in `Mathlib.RepresentationTheory.Maschke` (and used in `RepresentationTheory.FinGroupCharZero`).

annotations.label: Coherent sheaves on a Noetherian scheme

anchor.snippet: The category of coherent sheaves on a Noetherian scheme is semi-simple if and only if

annotations.note: No formalized category of coherent sheaves on a scheme (or characterization of its semi-simplicity) was found.

annotations.label: Representations of a Lie group (non-semi-simple)

anchor.snippet: the category of representations of the Lie group

annotations.note: No formalization of the non-semi-simplicity of finite-dimensional Lie group representations was found.

annotations.label: Exact subcategory

anchor.snippet: C is an exact subcategory if it is itself an exact category

annotations.note: No notion of exact subcategory (in the sense of Quillen exact structure on a full subcategory) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abelian subcategory

anchor.snippet: C is an abelian subcategory if it is itself an abelian category

annotations.note: No dedicated `AbelianSubcategory` predicate/class was found; one would express it ad-hoc via `Abelian` on the full subcategory plus exactness of the inclusion.

annotations.label: Thick subcategory

anchor.snippet: C is a thick subcategory if it is closed under taking direct summands

annotations.note: No `ThickSubcategory` class for abelian categories was found (the triangulated analogue exists but is a different notion).

annotations.label: Topologizing subcategory

anchor.snippet: C is a topologizing subcategory if it is closed under subquotients

annotations.note: No formalization of topologizing subcategories was found.

annotations.label: Serre subcategory

anchor.snippet: C is a Serre subcategory if, for all short exact sequences

annotations.note: `ObjectProperty.IsSerreClass` axiomatizes Serre classes/subcategories of an abelian category.

annotations.label: Localizing subcategory

anchor.snippet: C is a localizing subcategory if it is a Serre subcategory

annotations.note: No `LocalizingSubcategory` notion for abelian categories was found (the triangulated `IsVerdierLocalizing` is a different setting).

annotations.label: Wide subcategory

anchor.snippet: There are two competing notions of a wide subcategory

annotations.note: No formalization of wide subcategories (in either sense) was found.

annotations.label: Abelian full subcategory whose inclusion is not exact

anchor.snippet: Here is an explicit example of a full, additive subcategory of an abelian category that is itself abelian but the inclusion functor is not exact

annotations.note: No formalized counter-example of an abelian full subcategory with non-exact inclusion was located.
